title: Spike: evaluate zcash dep workarounds for core2 breakage
description: nie-m91 is deferred due to zcash_primitives 0.26.x / zcash_address 0.10.x being broken because all 0.3.x versions of transitive dep core2 are yanked. Three options must be evaluated: (a) git dep from zcash/librustzcash main branch (check whether their Cargo.toml has moved past core2), (b) in-house ZIP-32 child derivation using blake2b_simd + jubjub crates (no core2 dep), (c) wait for ECC to publish a fix to crates.io. Use TWO parallel subagents: subagent-A fetches and parses zcash/librustzcash main/Cargo.lock to check whether core2 0.4+ or a replacement is present; subagent-B spikes option (b) by grepping the jubjub and blake2b_simd crate docs for the API surface needed for ZIP-32 PRF^expand and key derivation. Both agents report findings; main agent writes a recommendation doc as output notes.
design: Do not touch Cargo.toml until recommendation is approved. Spike only.
acceptance_criteria: Written recommendation names one workaround with evidence. Cargo.lock or API surface excerpt included as proof. No code written yet.
notes:
## Spike Results (2026-04-18)

### Subagent A: librustzcash main branch status

librustzcash PR #2306 'corez_migration' just merged. The main branch now declares:
- zcash_primitives = "0.26.4" (same version series, post-migration)
- core2: ZERO occurrences in Cargo.lock
- corez = "0.1.1" pinned in workspace Cargo.toml; corez is on crates.io (not yanked)

Option (a) — git dep from librustzcash main — will resolve cleanly without any yanked crates.

### Subagent B: blake2b_simd + jubjub API surface

Both crates are available locally and have zero core2 transitive deps:
- blake2b_simd 1.0.4: supports personalization via Params::new().personal(b"ZcashIP32Sapling") — exactly what ZIP-32 needs
- jubjub 0.10.0: has Fr::from_bytes_wide(&[u8; 64]) — the key scalar reduction needed for ask/nsk derivation

Option (b) — in-house ZIP-32 — is feasible at ~120-150 lines of safe Rust.

### Recommendation: OPTION (A) — git dep from librustzcash main

Rationale:
1. Uses official ECC code — audited, battle-tested, has correct test vectors
2. Unblocks all wallet work NOW (no weeks waiting for crates.io release)
3. Drop path is clear: when ECC publishes 0.26.5 or 0.27.0 to crates.io, replace git dep with version dep in one line
4. Option (b) is tempting but writing our own crypto is higher risk — even simple BLAKE2b + scalar arithmetic can have subtle bugs (wrong byte ordering, wrong endianness on scalar reduction, off-by-one on PRF input)

Option (c) — wait — is rejected: unknown timeline, blocks all Phase 2 work.

### Git dep to use (when approved):

In workspace Cargo.toml:
```toml
[patch.crates-io]
zcash_primitives = { git = "https://github.com/zcash/librustzcash", rev = "d849151" }
zcash_address = { git = "https://github.com/zcash/librustzcash", rev = "d849151" }
```
(rev d849151 = the corez_migration merge commit tip)

### No code written — this is a spike. Cargo.toml not touched. Awaiting approval.
close_reason: Spike complete. Recommendation: use git dep from librustzcash main (PR #2306 corez_migration merged; core2 replaced by corez 0.1.1). Option (b) in-house is viable fallback (~120 lines). Option (c) wait rejected. See notes for full evidence and git dep snippet. Awaiting user approval before touching Cargo.toml.
